name: Vero API
what_it_does: Vero — behavioral email marketing platform that sends personalized emails based on user events and customer data, with a REST API for identifying users, tracking events, and managing campaigns.
use_cases: Agents tracking user behavioral events to trigger personalized email sequences based on in-app actions
Customer onboarding automation — agents sending triggered welcome and activation emails based on signup events
Event-driven campaigns — agents sending emails when users complete specific actions (first purchase, 30-day inactive)
User profile management — agents syncing customer properties to Vero for segmentation and personalization
Transactional + marketing unified — agents using Vero for both triggered transactional and marketing emails in one platform
not_for: E-commerce-specific email flows — Klaviyo is better optimized for Shopify/WooCommerce revenue attribution
Bulk broadcast campaigns without event triggers — Mailchimp or SendGrid are simpler for one-off newsletters
Very high-volume transactional email — use Postmark or SendGrid for infrastructure-scale transactional sends
best_when: Your agent product needs behavioral email automation triggered by user events and actions, with a developer-friendly API for event tracking.
avoid_when: You need e-commerce-specific automation or a modern visual flow builder — use Klaviyo or Customer.io.

auth.notes: Auth token from Vero account settings. Passed in all API requests. Account-level token — no scope granularity. Same token for all API operations.

pricing.paid_tiers: Essentials: $99/month (up to 25K subscribers); Growth: $250/month; Enterprise: custom
pricing.notes: Subscriber-based pricing. No free tier. 14-day trial available. Pricing competitive for behavioral email marketing platforms.

performance.rate_limits: Event tracking: no published rate limits; API: standard web service limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with basic documentation. Error responses include HTTP status and message. Documentation less comprehensive than competitors like Customer.io.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User identification is idempotent with same external_id. Events are not idempotent — duplicate events trigger duplicate emails. Email sends are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Duplicate event tracking triggers duplicate emails — agents must deduplicate events before sending
User external_id must be set before events are tracked — events without identified users may be dropped
Email sending is async — Vero queues sends; delivery timing depends on campaign scheduling configuration
Domain authentication required before any emails deliver — agents must verify DKIM/SPF in account settings
Vero documentation is older and may not reflect current API capabilities — test before building against edge cases

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR compliant. TLS enforced. US-only data processing. Single API key with no scope granularity. No SOC2 documentation. Older platform with less active security disclosures.
